brief: The International Monetary Fund has slightly reduced India's GDP growth forecast due to global tensions, including the US-Iran conflict, yet the country remains one of the fastest-growing economies. Meanwhile, Prime Minister Narendra Modi's visit to Australia is expected to enhance business relations between the two countries, and the Indian diaspora sees this as an opportunity to promote mutual trade and investment. On the economic front, experts believe that India's growth trajectory can accelerate through increased private investment, facilitated by policy reforms and coordination between the Centre and States, a sentiment echoed by the IMF's adjusted growth forecast for the country.

Geopolitics.title: US-Iran war impact: IMF lowers India’s GDP growth forecast marginally; still tags it among fastest growing economies
Geopolitics.summary: The International Monetary Fund has slightly reduced India's GDP growth forecast due to global tensions, including the US-Iran conflict. Despite this, India remains one of the fastest-growing economies. This adjustment reflects the potential impact of geopolitical events on India's economic growth.

Geopolitics.title: Global Leaders and Historic Anniversaries: A Look Ahead in July and August
Geopolitics.summary: Several significant global events are scheduled to take place in July and August, including diplomatic visits by world leaders, important EU and ASEAN meetings, and historical anniversaries. These events will mark milestones in global diplomacy and history. They are expected to shape international relations and cooperation in various regions.

Geopolitics.title: PM Modi's visit to Australia will improve business relations: Indian diaspora
Geopolitics.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi's visit to Australia is expected to enhance business relations between the two countries, according to the Indian diaspora. This visit aims to strengthen economic ties and cooperation between India and Australia. It is seen as an opportunity to promote mutual trade and investment.

Geopolitics.title: How Iran turned a state funeral into a masterclass in geopolitical messaging
Geopolitics.summary: Iran recently used a state funeral as an opportunity to convey specific geopolitical messages to international delegations. The event showcased Iran's ability to use diplomatic occasions for strategic messaging. This approach highlights the complex nature of international relations and diplomacy in the region.

Geopolitics.title: Italy's Citizenship Crackdown: New Bill Targets Foreign Offenders
Geopolitics.summary: The Italian government has proposed a bill to simplify the revocation of citizenship for foreign offenders and facilitate their repatriation. This move is part of a broader effort to tighten immigration laws and enhance domestic security. The bill has sparked discussions on human rights and international relations.

Economy.title: IMF lowers India’s FY27 growth outlook, retains status as fastest-growing economy
Economy.summary: The International Monetary Fund (IMF) has lowered India's growth outlook for FY27 to 6.4 percent, while still considering it the fastest-growing economy globally. Strong private consumption and services activity are expected to support this growth. The global growth forecast has been adjusted due to various factors, with the US economy expected to grow steadily and China's growth forecast upgraded.

Economy.title: India's Growth Hinges on Boosting Private Investment: Experts Urge Reforms and Coordination
Economy.summary: Experts believe that India's growth trajectory can accelerate through increased private investment, facilitated by policy reforms and coordination between the Centre and States. Deregulation and infrastructural advancements are seen as crucial elements in achieving the country's vision of a developed economy by 2047. Greater private investment is considered essential for India's growth.

Business.title: Neuberg readies for IPO in FY28, to seek up to Rs 4,000 cr
Business.summary: Neuberg Diagnostics is planning an initial public offering in fiscal year 2028 to raise approximately ₹3,500-4,000 crore. The company aims to use the funds for acquisitions and expansion, targeting pan-India growth and new diagnostic technologies. This move is expected to make Neuberg the second-largest player in the diagnostics market.

Business.title: Corporate Paper issuances touch 5-yr high at Rs 2.53 lakh crore in June
Business.summary: Indian companies raised ₹2.53 lakh crore through commercial papers in June, a near five-year high, driven by attractive pricing and seasonal needs. This surge reflects a shift from costlier bank loans to cheaper short-term market funding, supported by ample investor demand and a stable liquidity environment. The Reserve Bank of India's liquidity infusions also contributed to favorable conditions.

Business.title: India's banks are handing out more loans, but deposits aren't keeping up
Business.summary: Indian banks saw a surge in lending during the April-June quarter, outpacing deposit growth and widening the funding gap. This trend, driven by robust demand from corporate and retail sectors, has pushed the loan-to-deposit ratio to a decade-high, raising concerns about funding sustainability. The varied strategies of lenders have resulted in some seeing deposits decline, while others have maintained a balance.

Business.title: Operational, Says Report – Outlook Business
Business.summary: Nearly half of India's UDAN regional flight routes have become non-operational, despite the government launching a revamped phase with higher subsidies and airport funding. The viability gap support, infrastructure delays, and airline distress are affecting the country's flagship connectivity scheme. This development may impact the government's efforts to improve regional air connectivity.

Business.title: Inspira seals Rs 1,800-crore private credit for Burger King buy in India
Business.summary: Inspira Global has secured ₹1,800 crore in private credit to acquire a controlling stake in India's Burger King operator. The company has already raised ₹1,050 crore via NCDs and expects to raise the remaining ₹800 crore this month. This significant funding marks a major move into the quick-service restaurant sector for Inspira Global.

Business.title: Up Ecosystem on Foreign Capital, Family Offices May Now Have to Keep it Rolling – Outlook Business
Business.summary: India's family offices are emerging as key sources of patient capital for funding start-ups, innovation, and the country's next phase of entrepreneurial growth. As foreign capital becomes less reliable, family offices may need to step in to support the start-up ecosystem. This shift could have significant implications for the growth and development of Indian start-ups.

National Affairs.title: NCERT textbook revision: After Supreme Court rap over judiciary chapter, Hitler references removed, Congress' stance on Partition revised
National Affairs.summary: The NCERT has revised its Class 8 Social Science textbook after the Supreme Court ordered changes to be made, including the removal of references to Hitler and revisions to the Congress' stance on Partition. The revised textbook has been released after the Supreme Court's intervention. This change reflects the ongoing efforts to refine educational content in India.

National Affairs.title: TN moves SC against HC order striking down quota benefits for converts to Islam
National Affairs.summary: The state of Tamil Nadu has approached the Supreme Court to challenge a High Court order that struck down quota benefits for individuals who convert to Islam. The High Court had deemed the government order providing these benefits as unconstitutional. This case highlights the ongoing debates around reservation policies in India.

National Affairs.title: SC Says Lawyers Can't Be Blacklisted by Banks Over Negligence Allegations, Directs BCI to Explore National Legal Academy
National Affairs.summary: The Supreme Court has ruled that banks cannot blacklist lawyers based on allegations of professional negligence, stating that such actions infringe on the Bar Councils' jurisdiction. The Court has also directed the Bar Council of India to explore the establishment of a National Legal Academy. This decision aims to protect the rights of lawyers and promote a more regulated legal profession.

National Affairs.title: The fight to donate blood: Why queer Indians are challenging a lifetime ban
National Affairs.summary: A Supreme Court challenge has been filed against India's 2017 blood donor guidelines, which permanently exclude transgender persons, men who have sex with men, and female sex workers from donating blood. The challenge questions how a person's identity can determine their eligibility to donate blood. This case highlights the issues faced by the queer community in India.

National Affairs.title: External agency can't record findings against lawyer: SC
National Affairs.summary: The Supreme Court has stated that external agencies cannot record findings against lawyers, emphasizing the importance of the independence of the legal profession. This decision aims to protect lawyers from unfair assessments and promote the integrity of the legal system.

brief: Today's brief highlights significant developments across geopolitics, economy, and national affairs. The International Olympic Committee's decision to lift its ban on Russia, allowing the country's athletes to compete in the 2028 Olympics, has sparked attention amid the ongoing Ukraine war, with US President Donald Trump stating that he believes Russian President Vladimir Putin wants the conflict to end. Meanwhile, in India, the economy is undergoing significant reforms, but the country still has a long way to go in terms of changing its culture of governance, with concerns over a patchy monsoon season potentially leading to higher inflation and impacting the rural economy. Additionally, Indian companies have raised a substantial amount of funds through commercial papers, reaching a near five-year high, while the Supreme Court has made notable rulings on the rights of lawyers and the independence of the legal profession.

Geopolitics.title: IOC Lifts Ban On Russia; Athletes Cleared To Compete At LA 2028 Amid Ukraine War
Geopolitics.summary: The International Olympic Committee has lifted its ban on Russia, allowing Russian athletes to compete in the 2028 Olympics. However, the IOC has called for a decision on the use of the Russian flag and anthem to be made at a later date. This move comes amid the ongoing Ukraine war.

Geopolitics.title: China's Top 10 Threats: Tsinghua Report Reveals CCP's Concerns
Geopolitics.summary: A report by Tsinghua University's International Security and Strategy Center has outlined the top threats to China's national security, including Taiwan tensions, China-Japan relations, and the US-China tech divide. The report provides insight into China's geopolitical concerns and priorities. It highlights the complexities of China's international relationships and the challenges it faces.

Geopolitics.title: CWGC adds 9,909 overlooked Indian soldiers to First World War casualty records
Geopolitics.summary: The Commonwealth War Graves Commission has added nearly 10,000 Indian soldiers to its casualty records from the First World War, marking the largest single addition to its records since the Second World War. This move recognizes the significant contribution of Indian soldiers to the war effort and provides closure for their families. It also highlights the ongoing efforts to document and honor the sacrifices of soldiers from diverse backgrounds.

Geopolitics.title: Trump says Putin wants Ukraine war to end
Geopolitics.summary: US President Donald Trump has stated that he believes Russian President Vladimir Putin wants the Ukraine war to end. This statement comes amid ongoing diplomatic efforts to resolve the conflict. The comment reflects the complex and evolving nature of international relations and the search for a peaceful resolution to the war.

Geopolitics.title: Macron's Historic Visit to Post-Assad Syria
Geopolitics.summary: French President Emmanuel Macron has made a historic visit to Syria, marking the first visit by an EU head of state since the overthrow of Bashar al-Assad. The visit signifies a shift in international relations and Syria's efforts to rebuild and establish new ties with the global community. It also underscores the country's transformation under its new leadership.

Economy.title: Rupee slips as oil prices jump, modest portfolio inflows cushion fall
Economy.summary: The Indian rupee has weakened due to rising oil prices caused by Middle East hostilities, which could impact India's economic growth and inflation. However, modest portfolio inflows have helped cushion the fall. The situation is being closely watched as investors await the US Federal Reserve policy meeting minutes.

Economy.title: As India’s economy rises, Indian cities mustn’t fall behind on how liveable they are
Economy.summary: Indian cities rank low on the global urban liveability index, with Copenhagen ranking first. Experts suggest that a policy shift could help urban India improve its liveability. This is important as the country's economy continues to grow and its cities need to keep pace.

Economy.title: Reform Express: India’s economy is fast being transformed but we have a long journey ahead
Economy.summary: India's economy is undergoing significant reforms, but the country still has a long way to go in terms of changing its culture of governance. Effective implementation of these reforms is crucial to achieving the desired outcomes. The government's policy moves alone may not be enough to drive growth without corresponding changes in governance.

Economy.title: Patchy monsoon could lift inflation, hurt rural economy: S&P
Economy.summary: A patchy monsoon season could lead to higher food and energy prices, resulting in increased inflation in India. This may prompt the Reserve Bank of India to maintain a tight monetary policy stance. The rural economy is likely to be particularly affected by the monsoon's impact on agricultural production.

Economy.title: Experts Shed Light on E20 Fuel: Dispelling Myths and Highlighting Benefits
Economy.summary: Energy experts have clarified misconceptions about India's E20 ethanol-blended petrol mandate, stating that it has minimal impact on vehicle mileage and is compatible with older vehicles. The policy is expected to have economic and environmental benefits, including reduced emissions and improved air quality.

Business.title: Neuberg readies for IPO in FY28, to seek up to Rs 4,000 cr
Business.summary: Neuberg Diagnostics is planning an initial public offering in fiscal year 2028 to raise approximately ₹3,500-4,000 crore. The company aims to use the funds for acquisitions and expansion, targeting pan-India growth and new diagnostic technologies. This move is expected to make Neuberg the second-largest player in the diagnostics market.

Business.title: Corporate Paper issuances touch 5-yr high at Rs 2.53 lakh crore in June
Business.summary: Indian companies raised a significant amount of funds through commercial papers in June, reaching a near five-year high of Rs 2.53 lakh crore. This surge is attributed to attractive pricing, seasonal needs, and debt refinancing, with companies shifting from costlier bank loans to cheaper short-term market funding. The Reserve Bank of India's liquidity infusions also contributed to the favorable conditions.

Business.title: India's banks are handing out more loans, but deposits aren't keeping up
Business.summary: Indian banks have seen a surge in lending, outpacing deposit growth and widening the funding gap. The loan-to-deposit ratio has reached a decade-high, raising concerns about funding sustainability. This trend is driven by robust demand from corporate and retail sectors, with some lenders seeing deposits decline while others maintain a balance.

Business.title: Inspira seals Rs 1,800-crore private credit for Burger King buy in India
Business.summary: Inspira Global has secured ₹1,800 crore in private credit to acquire a controlling stake in India's Burger King operator. The company has already raised ₹1,050 crore via NCDs and expects to raise the remaining ₹800 crore this month. This significant funding marks a major move into the quick-service restaurant sector.

National Affairs.title: SC Says Lawyers Can't Be Blacklisted by Banks Over Negligence Allegations, Directs BCI to Explore National Legal Academy
National Affairs.summary: The Supreme Court has ruled that banks cannot blacklist lawyers based on allegations of professional negligence, as this infringes on the Bar Councils' jurisdiction. The court has also directed the Bar Council of India to explore setting up a National Legal Academy to provide continued legal education to lawyers. This decision aims to protect lawyers' rights and promote their professional development.

National Affairs.title: The fight to donate blood: Why queer Indians are challenging a lifetime ban
National Affairs.summary: Queer Indians are challenging the 2017 blood donor guidelines that permanently exclude transgender persons, men who have sex with men, and female sex workers from donating blood. The challenge is based on the question of whether a person's identity should determine their eligibility to donate blood. The issue is currently being considered by the Supreme Court.

National Affairs.title: External agency can't record findings against lawyer: SC
National Affairs.summary: The Supreme Court has stated that the independence of the legal profession is as important as the independence of the judiciary. The court ruled that external agencies cannot record findings against lawyers, emphasizing the need to maintain the autonomy of the legal profession.

National Affairs.title: Audit performance of disciplinary mechanism for lawyers: Supreme Court to Bar Council of India
National Affairs.summary: The Supreme Court has directed the Bar Council of India to audit the performance of its disciplinary mechanism for lawyers. The court has also suggested setting up a National Legal Academy to provide continued legal education to lawyers, aiming to enhance their professional competence and skills.

National Affairs.title: 'Can't use court as political platform': SC declines DMK's plea against Vijay
National Affairs.summary: The Supreme Court has dismissed a plea by the DMK seeking to restrain Tamil Nadu CM from making statements on a specific issue. The court stated that it cannot be used as a political platform, emphasizing the need to maintain the integrity of the judiciary.

The threats listed are significant as they have the potential to impact China's national security and global influence.

Geopolitics.title: CWGC adds 9,909 overlooked Indian soldiers to First World War casualty records

The addition is a significant step in recognizing the role of Indian soldiers in the war.

Geopolitics.title: Trump says Putin wants Ukraine war to end

This statement comes amid ongoing conflict in Ukraine and has implications for international relations and global security. The statement may indicate a potential shift in the diplomatic approach to resolving the conflict.

Geopolitics.title: Macron's Historic Visit to Post-Assad Syria

The visit signifies a shift in international relations with Syria under its new leadership. Macron's visit may pave the way for increased international engagement and cooperation with Syria.

Geopolitics.title: Balen Shah administration must impart new momentum to Nepal

Economy.summary: A patchy monsoon season could lead to higher food and energy prices, pushing inflation up to 5.1% in FY27, according to S&P. This may prompt the Reserve Bank of India to maintain a moderately tight monetary policy stance. The rural economy is likely to be particularly affected by the monsoon's impact on inflation.

Economy.title: Experts Shed Light on E20 Fuel: Dispelling Myths and Highlighting Benefits
Economy.summary: Energy industry experts have clarified misconceptions about India's E20 ethanol-blended petrol mandate, highlighting its minimal impact on vehicle mileage and addressing concerns over compatibility with older vehicles. The policy is expected to have economic and environmental benefits. The experts' insights aim to dispel myths surrounding the fuel.

Economy.title: Balancing Innovation and Stability: India's Progress on Digital Rupee Discussed
Economy.summary: The Parliamentary Standing Committee on Finance met with RBI officials and ICAI representatives to discuss India's progress on its Central Bank Digital Currency and the broader digital asset landscape. The meeting focused on regulatory challenges, taxation issues, and potential policy interventions to support India's digital financial ecosystem. The discussion aimed to balance innovation and stability in the sector.

Economy.title: RBI Kicks Off 74th Manufacturing Sector Survey: A Key Tool for Economic Analysis
Economy.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has launched its 74th Order Books, Inventories, and Capacity Utilisation Survey for the manufacturing sector, covering April-June 2026. The survey collects operational data to aid monetary policy and captures order volumes, inventory details, and capacity use. The initiative aims to provide valuable insights into the manufacturing sector's performance.

Business.title: Neuberg readies for IPO in FY28, to seek up to Rs 4,000 cr
Business.summary: Neuberg Diagnostics is planning an initial public offering in fiscal year 2028 to raise approximately ₹3,500-4,000 crore. The company aims to use the funds for acquisitions and expansion, targeting pan-India growth and new diagnostic technologies. This move is expected to make Neuberg the second-largest player in the diagnostics market.

Business.title: Corporate Paper issuances touch 5-yr high at Rs 2.53 lakh crore in June
Business.summary: Indian companies raised a significant amount of funds through commercial papers in June, reaching a near five-year high of Rs 2.53 lakh crore. This surge was driven by attractive pricing, seasonal needs, and debt refinancing, with companies shifting from costlier bank loans to cheaper short-term market funding. The Reserve Bank of India's liquidity infusions also contributed to favorable conditions.

Business.title: India's banks are handing out more loans, but deposits aren't keeping up
Business.summary: Indian banks have seen a surge in lending, outpacing deposit growth and widening the funding gap. This trend, driven by robust demand from corporate and retail sectors, has pushed the loan-to-deposit ratio to a decade-high, raising concerns about funding sustainability. The varied strategies of lenders have resulted in some seeing deposits decline, while others have maintained a balance.

Business.title: Operational, Says Report – Outlook Business
Business.summary: Nearly half of India's UDAN regional flight routes have become non-operational, despite the government launching a revamped phase with higher subsidies and airport funding. The viability gap support, infrastructure delays, and airline distress are affecting the country's flagship connectivity scheme. This development may impact the government's efforts to improve regional air connectivity.

Business.title: Inspira seals Rs 1,800-crore private credit for Burger King buy in India
Business.summary: Inspira Global has secured ₹1,800 crore in private credit to acquire a controlling stake in India's Burger King operator. The company has already raised ₹1,050 crore via NCDs and expects to raise the remaining ₹800 crore this month. This significant funding marks a major move into the quick-service restaurant sector.

Business.summary: India's family offices are emerging as key sources of patient capital for funding start-ups, innovation, and entrepreneurial growth. This decision is important as it clarifies the role of banks and financial institutions in relation to lawyers. The court's ruling will have implications for the legal profession in India.

National Affairs.title: CCI moves SC against NCLAT order quashing penalty on Grasim
National Affairs.summary: The Competition Commission of India (CCI) has moved the Supreme Court against a tribunal's decision to quash a ₹300-crore penalty on Grasim Industries for alleged monopolistic behavior. The CCI is seeking to restore its previous decision against the company. This case is significant as it relates to the regulation of monopolistic practices in India's viscose staple fibre market.

National Affairs.title: Asian Games: SC to Hear Plea Against HC's Refusal to Interfere with Dressage Team Selection
National Affairs.summary: The Supreme Court will hear a plea challenging the Delhi High Court's refusal to intervene in the selection of India's dressage team for the Asian Games. This meeting is crucial for shaping India's digital financial ecosystem. The discussion aims to balance innovation and stability in the country's digital currency landscape.

Economy.title: RBI Kicks Off 74th Manufacturing Sector Survey: A Key Tool for Economic Analysis
Economy.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has launched its 74th survey for the manufacturing sector, covering April-June 2026, to collect operational data for monetary policy. The survey will capture order volumes, inventory details, and capacity use, aiding economic analysis. This initiative is a key tool for understanding the manufacturing sector's performance and its impact on the economy.

Economy.title: Nomura, HSBC split on RBI rate outlook as weak monsoon fuels inflation risks
Economy.summary: Economists from Nomura and HSBC have differing views on the RBI's next policy move due to a weak monsoon, which may fuel inflation risks. They assess the impact on India's economy, including GDP growth, food inflation, and the rupee. This split in outlook reflects the uncertainty surrounding the RBI's future actions.

Economy.title: Rupee's defence takes RBI dollar shorts to a record high
Economy.summary: The Reserve Bank of India's net short forward position in the currency market has surged to a record $106.6 billion in May, indicating significant interventions to shield the rupee. The central bank faces a decision on whether to reduce its forward position or bolster reserves, as the rupee has recovered from its record low due to capital inflows and policy measures.

Economy.title: India Inc eyes more dollar debt as RBI policy support kicks in
Economy.summary: Indian firms are increasingly opting for dollar debt due to the RBI's moves to lower hedging costs and ease rules. This shift is driven by central bank policies in February and June, which have made offshore funds more attractive than rupee loans. As a result, local firms are aggressively swapping out rupee loans for external commercial borrowings.

Business.title: India's banks are handing out more loans, but deposits aren't keeping up
Business.summary: Indian banks have seen a surge in lending, outpacing deposit growth and widening the funding gap, with the loan-to-deposit ratio reaching a decade-high. This trend is driven by demand from corporate and retail sectors, raising concerns about funding sustainability. The varied strategies of lenders have resulted in some deposits declining while others have maintained a balance.

Business.title: Operational, Says Report – Outlook Business
Business.summary: Almost half of India's UDAN regional flight routes have become non-operational, despite the government launching a revamped phase with higher subsidies and airport funding. The non-operational routes are due to viability gap support, infrastructure delays, and airline distress, which are reshaping the country's flagship connectivity scheme. This development affects the country's regional air connectivity.

Business.title: Inspira seals Rs 1,800-crore private credit for Burger King buy in India
Business.summary: Inspira Global has secured ₹1,800 crore in private credit to acquire a controlling stake in India's Burger King operator. The funding is a significant move into the quick-service restaurant sector, with ₹1,050 crore already raised via NCDs and the remaining ₹800 crore expected to be raised this month. This deal highlights the growing interest in India's food business sector.

Business.title: Up Ecosystem on Foreign Capital, Family Offices May Now Have to Keep it Rolling – Outlook Business
Business.summary: India's family offices are emerging as key sources of patient capital for funding start-ups, innovation, and the country's next phase of entrepreneurial growth. This development is crucial as India's start-up ecosystem has been largely dependent on foreign capital. Family offices are now expected to play a significant role in sustaining the ecosystem.

Business.title: India's private credit market primed for sustained growth
Business.summary: India's private credit market is expected to reach $50 billion by 2030, driven by increasing corporate funding needs and limitations faced by traditional banks. The real estate and infrastructure sectors are leading this growth, with promoter financing also playing a crucial role. New RBI regulations allowing bank acquisition financing may introduce greater competition in the market.

National Affairs.title: Mumbai To Get India’s First City-Wide Master Pedestrian Network Plan, BMC Tells Supreme Court
National Affairs.summary: The BMC is creating India's first city-wide master pedestrian network plan for Mumbai, aiming to create a continuous and accessible footpath network and improve connectivity with public transport. This plan uses advanced technologies like GIS, LiDAR, and drone mapping. The Supreme Court will hear the matter next on September 3.

National Affairs.title: Supreme Court Sounds Alarm On Overdependence On AI For Analyses On Legal Matters
National Affairs.summary: The Supreme Court has warned against overreliance on artificial intelligence in legal reasoning, citing instances where fake precedents generated by AI influenced court orders. The court has directed the Bar Council of India to frame guidelines and disciplinary measures to prevent misuse of AI in courts. This move aims to ensure the integrity of the legal process.

National Affairs.title: Inaccurate to say passport is only travel document, says former Supreme Court judge Madan Lokur
National Affairs.summary: Former Supreme Court judge Madan Lokur has stated that it is inaccurate to say a passport is only a travel document, suggesting that it is also a proof of citizenship. He emphasized the need to clarify the legal position that a person holding an Indian passport is a citizen of India. This clarification is important for understanding the rights and privileges associated with Indian citizenship.

National Affairs.title: The right to walk: After Supreme Court judgement, who will reclaim our footpaths?
National Affairs.summary: A recent Supreme Court judgment has highlighted the importance of pedestrian infrastructure, following the death of a child who was killed by a tanker while walking to school due to the absence of a footpath. The judgment emphasizes the need for reclaiming footpaths and ensuring the right to walk safely. This issue is crucial for urban planning and pedestrian safety in India.

National Affairs.title: Shri Krishna Janmabhoomi-Shahi Idgah dispute: Hindu side attends Lok Adalat, proposes relocation of mosque
National Affairs.summary: A special Lok Adalat was held in Mathura as part of the Supreme Court's efforts to resolve the Shri Krishna Janmabhoomi-Shahi Idgah dispute amicably. The Hindu side attended the Lok Adalat and proposed the relocation of the mosque. This development is part of the ongoing efforts to find a peaceful resolution to the dispute.

Meanwhile, in India, the economy is witnessing significant trends, including the RBI's efforts to support the economy through policy moves, which have led to Indian companies opting for dollar debt, and the launch of the 74th manufacturing sector survey to aid monetary policy decisions. Additionally, the business sector is seeing growth in private credit, with India's family offices emerging as key sources of patient capital for start-ups and entrepreneurial growth.

Geopolitics.title: I don't think it is possible: Keir Starmer says next PM cannot spend less time on diplomacy
Geopolitics.summary: Outgoing UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer has defended the time he spent on international affairs, stating that the next PM cannot afford to spend less time on diplomacy. This highlights the importance of diplomacy in a leader's role. Starmer's comments come as he prepares to leave office, emphasizing the need for a balance between domestic and international focus.

Geopolitics.title: ISI's new war on India targets PM Modi with deepfakes and disinformation
Geopolitics.summary: India's national security planners have detected a new campaign by Pakistan's Inter Services Intelligence (ISI) targeting Prime Minister Narendra Modi with deepfakes and disinformation. This is a significant development in the geopolitical landscape, highlighting the use of emerging technologies in information warfare. The campaign underscores the ongoing tensions between India and Pakistan.

Geopolitics.title: Iran vows legal action against US, Israel over war, seeks global accountability
Geopolitics.summary: Iran has announced plans to take legal action against the US and Israel over their military campaign, which Tehran claims is a breach of international law. The Iranian government will use diplomatic and legal mechanisms to seek accountability. This development highlights the escalating tensions in the Middle East and the role of international law in geopolitical conflicts.

Geopolitics.title: Iran bids farewell to Khamenei; burial on July 9, Mojtaba's attendance uncertain
Geopolitics.summary: Iran is mourning the death of its supreme leader, Ali Khamenei, with week-long ceremonies and a grand burial procession. The event has significant geopolitical implications, as it may impact the country's leadership and international relations. The attendance of Khamenei's son, Mojtaba, who is seen as a potential successor, is uncertain, adding to the speculation about Iran's future.

Economy.title: Experts Shed Light on E20 Fuel: Dispelling Myths and Highlighting Benefits
Economy.summary: Energy industry experts have clarified misconceptions about India's E20 ethanol-blended petrol mandate, highlighting its minimal impact on vehicle mileage and emphasizing its economic and environmental benefits. The experts also addressed concerns over older vehicle compatibility. This development is significant as India moves towards a more sustainable fuel economy.

Economy.title: Balancing Innovation and Stability: India's Progress on Digital Rupee Discussed
Economy.summary: The Parliamentary Standing Committee on Finance met with RBI officials to review India's Central Bank Digital Currency progress and discuss regulatory challenges, taxation issues, and potential policy interventions. This meeting is crucial for shaping India's digital financial ecosystem. The discussion aims to strike a balance between innovation and stability in the sector.

Economy.title: RBI Kicks Off 74th Manufacturing Sector Survey: A Key Tool for Economic Analysis
Economy.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has launched its 74th survey for the manufacturing sector, covering April-June 2026, to collect operational data that will aid monetary policy decisions. The survey captures order volumes, inventory details, and capacity use, and is open to wide industry participation. This initiative is a key tool for economic analysis and will help the RBI make informed decisions.

Economy.title: Nomura, HSBC split on RBI rate outlook as weak monsoon fuels inflation risks
Economy.summary: Economists from Nomura and HSBC have differing views on the RBI's next policy move due to the impact of a weak monsoon on India's economy. They assess the effects on GDP growth, food inflation, the rupee, and balance of payments. This split in opinion highlights the uncertainty surrounding the RBI's rate outlook and the challenges posed by a weak monsoon.

Economy.title: Rupee's defence takes RBI dollar shorts to a record high
Economy.summary: The Reserve Bank of India's net short forward position in the currency market has surged to a record $106.6 billion in May, indicating significant interventions to shield the rupee from hitting new lows. The central bank now faces a decision on whether to reduce its forward position or bolster reserves, as the rupee has recovered from its record low due to capital inflows and policy measures.

Economy.title: India Inc eyes more dollar debt as RBI policy support kicks in
Economy.summary: Indian companies are increasingly opting for dollar debt due to the RBI's recent policy moves, which have lowered hedging costs and eased rules. This shift is driven by the central bank's efforts to support the economy, and companies are aggressively swapping out rupee loans for offshore funds. This trend is significant for India's external borrowing landscape.

Business.title: India's banks are handing out more loans, but deposits aren't keeping up
Business.summary: Indian banks have seen a surge in lending, outpacing deposit growth and widening the funding gap, with the loan-to-deposit ratio reaching a decade-high. This trend is driven by demand from corporate and retail sectors, raising concerns about funding sustainability. The varied strategies of lenders have resulted in some deposits declining while others have maintained a balance.

Business.title: Almost half of UDAN routes have become non-operational
Business.summary: Nearly half of India's UDAN regional flight routes are no longer operational due to viability gap support, infrastructure delays, and airline distress. The government has launched a revamped phase with higher subsidies and airport funding. This development affects the country's flagship connectivity scheme, which aims to improve regional air connectivity.

Business.title: Inspira seals Rs 1,800-crore private credit for Burger King buy in India
Business.summary: Inspira Global has secured ₹1,800 crore in private credit to acquire a controlling stake in India's Burger King operator. The company has already raised ₹1,050 crore via NCDs and expects to raise the remaining ₹800 crore this month. This significant funding marks a major move into the quick-service restaurant sector.

Business.title: India's family offices emerge as key sources of patient capital
Business.summary: India's family offices are becoming a crucial source of patient capital for funding start-ups, innovation, and entrepreneurial growth. They are expected to play a significant role in sustaining the country's start-up ecosystem, which was initially built on foreign capital. Family offices can provide long-term funding, which is essential for the growth of start-ups.

Business.title: India's private credit market primed for sustained growth
Business.summary: India's private credit market is expected to reach $50 billion by 2030, driven by increasing corporate funding needs and limitations faced by traditional banks. The real estate and infrastructure sectors are leading this growth, with promoter financing also playing a crucial role. New RBI regulations allowing bank acquisition financing may introduce greater competition in the market.

National Affairs.title: RJD MP Moves Supreme Court Seeking CBI Probe, Forensic Audit Of Ram Janmabhoomi Trust Finances
National Affairs.summary: RJD MP Sudhakar Singh has moved the Supreme Court seeking a CBI probe into the financial affairs of the Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust over alleged donation misappropriation. The petition also seeks a forensic audit and an oversight committee to ensure transparency. This matter is significant as it raises questions about the trust's financial management and transparency.

National Affairs.title: Delhi Gymkhana Club challenges Centre's eviction proceedings in HC, plea listed for July 6
National Affairs.summary: The Delhi Gymkhana Club has moved the Delhi High Court against the government's decision to initiate eviction proceedings. The club's plea is listed for hearing on July 6. This case is notable as it involves a dispute between a prestigious club and the government over the use of land.

National Affairs.title: DMK moves SC in Karur stampede case, raises concerns on probe
National Affairs.summary: The DMK has moved the Supreme Court seeking to be impleaded as a party in the Karur stampede case, citing concerns about the fairness and independence of the CBI investigation. The party's intervention is aimed at ensuring a thorough and unbiased probe. This development is significant as it highlights the ongoing investigation into the stampede and the DMK's efforts to ensure accountability.

National Affairs.title: SC critical of HC order but refuses to stay Sonam bail
National Affairs.summary: The Supreme Court has expressed criticism of a Meghalaya High Court order granting bail to Sonam Raghu on technical grounds. However, the apex court has refused to stay the bail order. This case is notable as it involves a dispute over the grant of bail and the Supreme Court's scrutiny of lower court decisions.

National Affairs.summary: India's Attorney General has denied reports that the Centre described its E20 fuel programme as an 'experiment' before the Supreme Court. The programme aims to blend 20% ethanol with petrol. The Attorney General's clarification is significant as it addresses concerns about the government's commitment to the programme and its potential environmental impact.

brief: Today's brief highlights significant developments across geopolitics, economy, and national affairs. Iran's restricted access to international inspectors and its push for a strategic partnership with China signal escalating tensions in the Middle East, while the US and Iran engage in indirect talks to prevent military escalation. In India, the Reserve Bank of India's efforts to shield the rupee from global tensions and the government's commitment to its E20 fuel programme are notable, as the country navigates economic and environmental challenges. Meanwhile, Indian companies are increasingly taking on dollar debt and the startup ecosystem is poised for growth, with a focus on DeepTech and AI ventures, amidst regulatory changes and potential impacts on venture capital funding.

Geopolitics.title: Iran Restricts Inspector Access to Two Sites, Ghalibaf Denies Broader Entry Claims
Geopolitics.summary: Iran has restricted international inspector access to only two sites, the Bushehr Power Plant and the Tehran Reactor, with no further access permitted. Iran's Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf has also announced a visit to China to elevate Tehran-Beijing relations to a strategic partnership. This move signals a significant diplomatic push by Iran amidst ongoing tensions with the US.

Geopolitics.title: US-Iran Peace Deal Live: Strait of Hormuz to remain 'Warlike Operations Area' until July 9
Geopolitics.summary: The US and Iran are holding indirect talks in Qatar to prevent another military escalation in the Middle East. The discussions focus on restoring commercial shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, securing a ceasefire, and implementing a 14-point interim accord. The talks also aim to address Iran's nuclear program and the release of frozen assets.

Geopolitics.title: Indian diplomat DB Venkatesh Varma chairs UNSG advisory body on disarmament
Geopolitics.summary: Indian diplomat DB Venkatesh Varma has become the first Indian to chair the UN Secretary-General's Advisory Board on Disarmament Matters. This leadership role highlights India's growing influence in global security dialogues, particularly amidst ongoing international conflicts. The board convened in New York, with Varma leading discussions on disarmament.

Geopolitics.title: 'Iran's nuclear rights are non-negotiable': Iran's Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf says key commitments under MoU need to be fulfilled
Geopolitics.summary: Iran's Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf has stated that Iran's nuclear rights are non-negotiable and that the country will not proceed with further talks until key commitments are met. These commitments include ending the war in Lebanon and ensuring passage through the Strait of Hormuz. Ghalibaf emphasized Iran's adherence to the NPT and IAEA supervision.

Geopolitics.title: China using 'two-faced diplomacy' in Central Africa: Report
Geopolitics.summary: A report has accused China of using 'two-faced diplomacy' in Central Africa, where Beijing publicly speaks about peace and economic cooperation while aggressively positioning its military. This approach has raised concerns about China's intentions in the region. The report highlights the complexities of China's diplomatic efforts in Central Africa.

Economy.title: RBI Kicks Off 74th Manufacturing Sector Survey: A Key Tool for Economic Analysis
Economy.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has launched its 74th survey for the manufacturing sector, which collects operational data to aid monetary policy. The survey will capture order volumes, inventory details, and capacity use, and is open to wide industry participation. This data will be crucial for understanding the current state of the manufacturing sector and informing economic decisions.

Economy.title: Nomura, HSBC split on RBI rate outlook as weak monsoon fuels inflation risks
Economy.summary: Economists from Nomura and HSBC have differing views on the RBI's next policy move due to the impact of a weak monsoon on India's economy. A weak monsoon could lead to higher food inflation, affecting GDP growth and the rupee. The RBI's decision will be crucial in managing these risks and maintaining economic stability.

Economy.title: Rupee's defence takes RBI dollar shorts to a record high
Economy.summary: The Reserve Bank of India's net short forward position in the currency market has reached a record high, indicating significant interventions to shield the rupee from global tensions. The central bank faces a decision on whether to reduce its forward position or bolster reserves, which will impact the rupee's value and the economy. The rupee has recovered from its record low due to capital inflows and policy measures.

Economy.title: India Inc eyes more dollar debt as RBI policy support kicks in
Economy.summary: Indian companies are increasingly taking on dollar debt due to recent policy changes by the RBI, which have lowered hedging costs and eased rules. This shift from rupee loans to offshore funds is driven by the central bank's moves to support the economy. The change in borrowing patterns could have significant implications for India's external debt and the rupee.

Economy.title: Easing oil prices, supply chains to soften external pressures on India: Finance ministry
Economy.summary: The Indian economy remains resilient despite global uncertainties, with easing oil prices and improving supply chains offering some relief. However, risks such as monsoon vagaries and geopolitical shifts in West Asia still pose a threat to growth and inflation. The finance ministry is emphasizing the need for proactive policy measures, including building national buffer stocks and reorienting farm policies.

Economy.title: Let's gilt trip those foreign investors
Economy.summary: Goldman Sachs has highlighted Indian government bonds as a prime investment opportunity for foreign investors, citing favorable policy changes and a strong economic recovery. The Indian bond market is attracting significant overseas interest due to its robust domestic consumption, strategic capital expenditure, and attractive yields. This could provide a compelling avenue for diversification and investment for global institutions.

Business.title: Debt's the way for Indian IT acquisition funding
Business.summary: Indian IT companies are increasingly using debt financing for acquisitions, as seen in Persistent Systems' $1.5-billion bridge financing from Barclays for its proposed acquisition of German IT firm Nagarro. This trend highlights the growing importance of debt financing in the Indian IT sector. The use of debt financing allows companies to pursue larger acquisitions and expand their operations.

Business.title: Intel exec Raja Koduri's OXMIQ raises $35 million to build licensable AI chip architecture
Business.summary: OXMIQ, a startup founded by former Intel executive Raja Koduri, has raised $35 million in Series A funding to develop licensable AI chip architecture. The company aims to reduce the high cost of AI compute, particularly for emerging markets like India and Southeast Asia, by allowing businesses and governments to create custom AI processors. This move could foster wider AI adoption in these regions.

Business.title: Stricter digital rules could cost Indian startups Rs 91,500 crore in annual VC funding: Oxford Economics
Business.summary: A study by Oxford Economics suggests that stricter digital regulations in India could result in a significant decline in venture capital funding for startups, with potential losses of Rs 91,500 crore annually. This could also lead to a reduction in the number of startups and startup jobs in the country. The study highlights the potential impact of regulatory changes on the Indian startup ecosystem.

Business.title: Renfra Energy India Files DRHP With SEBI For ₹430 Crore IPO
Business.summary: Renfra Energy India has filed a draft red herring prospectus with the Securities and Exchange Board of India for an initial public offering of ₹430 crore. The company plans to use the proceeds to repay debt, fund working capital, and support expansion plans. The IPO is expected to help the company grow its renewable energy business.

Business.title: Avendus Wealth-Hurun India Under 30 List 2026 Unveiled; DeepTech & HardTech Dominate India’s Startup Landscape
Business.summary: The Avendus Wealth-Hurun India Under 30 List 2026 highlights the growing importance of DeepTech and AI ventures in India's startup ecosystem. The list features 102 young Indian entrepreneurs with a combined valuation of ₹2.9 lakh crore, demonstrating the potential of the country's startup sector. Bengaluru leads the startup hubs, and most founders are first-generation entrepreneurs.

National Affairs.title: Tamil Nadu govt moves SC against Madras HC order banning cow slaughter
National Affairs.summary: The Tamil Nadu government has approached the Supreme Court to challenge a Madras High Court order that imposed a blanket ban on cow slaughter in the state. The move is significant as it highlights the ongoing debate around cow protection laws in India. The Supreme Court's decision will have implications for the state's laws and policies related to animal slaughter.

National Affairs.title: No default bail if chargesheet filed but not provided to accused: Supreme Court
National Affairs.summary: The Supreme Court has ruled that an accused person cannot claim default bail if the chargesheet has been filed in court but not provided to them. This decision clarifies the procedure for default bail and has implications for the criminal justice system in India. The ruling aims to prevent misuse of the default bail provision.

National Affairs.title: Supreme Court lays down ITR norms for fixing accident damages
National Affairs.summary: The Supreme Court has laid down guidelines for using income tax returns (ITR) to determine the income of individuals in cases of accident damages. The court noted that the income of salaried and self-employed individuals should be assessed differently. This decision will help in calculating fair compensation for accident victims.

National Affairs.title: AG Rejects Reports That Centre Described E20 Fuel Programme As An ‘Experiment’
National Affairs.summary: The Attorney General has denied reports that the Centre described its E20 fuel programme as an 'experiment' before the Supreme Court. The programme aims to blend 20% ethanol with petrol to reduce emissions. The clarification is significant as it underscores the government's commitment to the programme and its potential impact on the environment and energy sector.

This move aims to protect investors and maintain the integrity of India's capital markets.

The Supreme Court's decision will have implications for cow slaughter laws in the state.

National Affairs.title: NSE is a public authority under RTI Act, rules Delhi High Court
National Affairs.summary: The Delhi High Court has ruled that the National Stock Exchange (NSE) is a 'public authority' under the Right to Information (RTI) Act. This means the NSE will be subject to transparency laws, making it accountable to provide information to the public. The decision is significant as it increases transparency in India's largest stock exchange.
